description_long: This apartment building features a blocky, modular design with three main connected structures of varying heights, each topped with flat roofs. The exterior is finished in a uniform, muted beige or light brown, giving it a utilitarian and somewhat modern appearance. There are multiple sets of stairs visible, both external and leading to rooftop areas, suggesting access to different levels and possibly communal rooftop spaces. Small, regularly spaced windows punctuate the facades, and a covered entrance or porch is present on one side. The building is accompanied by a small cylindrical structure, possibly a utility or ventilation shaft. The overall design is functional, with minimal ornamentation and a focus on geometric forms.
description: A blocky, beige apartment building with flat roofs, external stairs, and small, regularly spaced windows. The design is utilitarian and modern, with minimal ornamentation.
description_view_0: Side view showing the narrow profile of the building, external stairs, and a small cylindrical structure adjacent to the main building.
description_view_1: Rear or side view displaying three distinct building sections, each with flat roofs and visible window arrangements; the covered entrance is also visible.
description_view_2: Top-down view highlighting the flat roofs, rooftop access points, and the arrangement of the three main building sections.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the main staircases, window placements, and the overall stepped profile of the building.
attribute_window_arrangement: Windows are small, regularly spaced, and arranged in horizontal rows across the facades, providing a sense of symmetry and uniformity.
